headline: Domino’s (NASDAQ:DPZ) Q2 CY2026: Beats On Revenue, Stock Soars
summary: Fast-food pizza chain Domino’s (NASDAQ:DPZ) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, with sales up 4.3% year on year to $1.19 billion. Its GAAP profit of $4.07 per share was 2.4% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Domino’s Pizza Revenue Grows, as Same-Store Sales Remain Pressured
summary: Domino’s Pizza  reported second-quarter results on Monday that beat Wall Street’s expectation on revenue, but missed the bar for earnings.  For the quarter that ended June 14, the pizza chain posted total revenue of $1.19 billion, up 4.3% from the same period a year ago and above Wall Street’s expectation of $1.18 billion.  Favorable currency movements further increased the value of the overseas revenue when converted into U.S. dollars.

content.title: Domino’s Pizza Revenue Grows, as Same-Store Sales Remain Pressured
content.summary: Domino’s Pizza  reported second-quarter results on Monday that beat Wall Street’s expectation on revenue, but missed the bar for earnings.  For the quarter that ended June 14, the pizza chain posted total revenue of $1.19 billion, up 4.3% from the same period a year ago and above Wall Street’s expectation of $1.18 billion.  Favorable currency movements further increased the value of the overseas revenue when converted into U.S. dollars.

headline: Wall Street futures rise, oil prices retreat despite an escalation in fighting by the US and Iran
summary: Wall Street futures were rising in premarket trading Monday and oil prices retreated, but not before a gallon of gas in the U.S. breached the $4 level again.  Futures for the S&P 500 rose 0.6%, while Nasdaq futures jumped 1.1%.  The company fell short of Wall Street's profit targets, even as its net income grew 3.6% over the same period last year.

content.title: Wall Street futures rise, oil prices retreat despite an escalation in fighting by the US and Iran
content.summary: Wall Street futures were rising in premarket trading Monday and oil prices retreated, but not before a gallon of gas in the U.S. breached the $4 level again.
